evidence: Delta-scoped write-time invariant gate verified on merged main. Gates: build / skills / check / full bounded test suite exit 0 — notably, the gate went live across every op-calling test in the suite with ZERO regressions, which is the delta scope proving itself against pre-existing fixture states. Four cells green: error-introduction refused with EVERY top-level substrate *.json byte-restored; a pre-existing violation on a legacy-shaped substrate never blocks an unrelated write; warning-introduction succeeds with the warning surfaced on the result and the write landed; and the shared-helper pin (the gate's classification deepEqual to validateContext's for the same state — the identical-verdict guarantee enforced by test). Runtime demonstration on the LIVE substrate (tsx against built dist): update-block-item attempting TASK-021 -> completed with no verification edge was REFUSED naming completed-task-has-verification and tasks.json read back byte-identical — the canonical verification-before-completion discipline enforced by the engine at the causal write.

criteria_results.criterion: Completing a task that addresses a non-complete feature surfaces the warning ON THE WRITE'S RESULT; an error-severity introduction refuses the write with the file byte-unchanged
criteria_results.evidence: warning cell (write-warning line on the success string, write landed) + error cell (refusal message names the violation, all files byte-restored) + the live-substrate refusal demo

criteria_results.criterion: Delta-scoping proven: pre-existing instances never warn or block; only newly-introduced violations act
